lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Here, take this enchanted symbol of the gods. It will guide you to the creatures.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You'll need to free up an inventory space before we continue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find these beings?

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: They can all be found in the Wilderness. The enchanted symbol can track their unique magical energies, leading you to their exact locations.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Be warned though, the symbol will take a blood sacrifice every time it's used.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any advice on fighting the beings?

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: They can't be harmed by normal magic, melee or ranged attacks, you will need to use the god combat spell corresponding to the creature you are fighting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about these beings.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: One is a justiciar. According to legend, the justiciars were the elite of Saradomin's forces. Some worked alone, operating as assassins behind enemy lines. Others led entire armies in Saradomin's name.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: I couldn't tell you why this one wanders the Wilderness, but I suspect he has good reason.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: The second is a Demon of Zamorak. From what I can tell, he is completely mad. He roams the Wilderness, killing all who he comes across without mercy. Even his own kind do not escape his fury.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: He's known to battle the justiciar quite regularly, perhaps they are old enemies.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: The final being is a Guthixian Ent. Like most followers of Guthix, he will avoid combat if possible. Make no mistake though, he is deadly when threatened.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: He seems to take great interest in the other two creatures. Maybe he is trying to keep a balance between them.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: All three of them give off a unique magical energy, one unlike any I have ever felt before. The fact that the three of them are so different, yet possess powers so similar, is very odd indeed.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: I'd be very interested in learning more about the source of their power.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, bye.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Farewell, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the remains of the Saradominist justiciar.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name], but we aren't finished yet.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Bring me the remains of all three magical beings. Do this and I will imbue their combined power into a single god cape of your choice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the remains of the Guthixian Ent.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name], but we aren't finished yet.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Bring me the remains of all three magical beings. Do this and I will imbue their combined power into a single god cape of your choice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the remains of the Zamorakian Demon.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name], but we aren't finished yet.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Bring me the remains of all three magical beings. Do this and I will imbue their combined power into a single god cape of your choice.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name], now you just need to hand me the god cape you wish to have imbued with great power.

sections.title: Using unimbued god capes on Kolodion

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: If you would like me to imbue any more capes, just bring me the remains of the respective creature.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You'll have to bring me the Guthix creature's remains to imbue this cape.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: If you would like me to imbue any more capes, just bring me the remains of the respective creature.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You'll have to bring me the Saradomin creature's remains to imbue this cape.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: If you would like me to imbue any more capes, just bring me the remains of the respective creature.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You'll have to bring me the Zamorak creature's remains to imbue this cape.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Kolodion.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Hey there, how are you? Are you enjoying the bloodshed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've had enough for now.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: A shame. You're a good battle mage. I hope to see you soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I use my new spells outside of the arena?

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Experience, my friend, experience. Once you've used the spell enough times in the arena, you'll be able to use them in the rest of Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good stuff.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Not so good for the citizens; they won't stand a chance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I doing so far?

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You still need to train with the strike spell inside the arena before you can use it outside.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You have completed over half of the training needed to use the strike spell outside of the arena.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You are almost ready to use the strike spell outside of the arena.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You're experienced enough to use the strike spell anywhere.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You still need to train with the claw spell inside the arena before you can use it outside.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You have completed over half of the training needed to use the claw spell outside of the arena.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You are almost ready to use the claw spell outside of the arena.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You're experienced enough to use the claw spell anywhere.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You still need to train with the flame spell inside the arena before you can use it outside.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You have completed over half of the training needed to use the flame spell outside of the arena.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You are almost ready to use the flame spell outside of the arena.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You're experienced enough to use the flame spell anywhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are there any more challenges available?

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You still need to learn how to cast all god spells outside of the arena.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: I've made a note of the spells you still need to learn in your journal.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You have indeed shown potential, [player name].

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: However, a wizard of your level wouldn't stand a chance.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: I am one of the most powerful mages in existence. But even my power has limitations. There are some beings however that have power exceeding even my own.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: I have detected three life forces within the Wilderness, each of them giving out a strange magical power, one unlike any I have ever felt before.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: If we could harness the power of these beings, I could imbue your god cape with that power, greatly increasing its potential.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I've been waiting for an improvement!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: I will need you to kill these three beings and bring me their remains. Even in death, their power should be great enough for me to harness it for our own uses.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where am I supposed to find these beings?

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Here, take this enchanted symbol of the gods. It will guide you to the creatures.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Bring me the remains of all three magical beings. Do this and I will imbue their combined power into a single god cape of your choice.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You'll need to free up an inventory space before we continue.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: I see... If you ever change your mind, you know where to find me.

sections.title: Talking to Kolodion after the mini-quest
If remains have been given to Kolodion but not yet used to redeem a cape

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You may currently have a Saradomin cape, a Guthix cape and a Zamorak cape imbued, just hand over the cape when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: Here, take this enchanted symbol of the gods. It will guide you to the creatures.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: You'll need to free up an inventory space before we continue.

lines.speaker: Kolodion
lines.text: If you would like me to imbue any more capes, just bring me the remains of the respective creature.
